name: Attack
scalingSummary: Extra Attack class features grant additional attacks per Attack action
mechanicalSummary: Make one melee or ranged weapon attack, or an unarmed strike. Characters with Extra Attack can make multiple attacks when taking this action.

evidence.pointer: CombatAction definitions per class; Extra Attack features in level progression

notes: Replaces one weapon attack when used for grapple or shove (see grapple and shove entries).

name: Cast a Spell
resourceCostSummary: Expends a spell slot of the spell's level or higher (cantrips cost no slot)
rechargeSummary: Spell slots recharge on a long rest (pact magic on short rest; Arcane Recovery on short rest)
scalingSummary: Many spells can be cast using a higher-level slot for increased effect
mechanicalSummary: Cast a spell with a casting time of 1 action. Requires any verbal, somatic, or material components. If the casting time is a bonus action or reaction, the appropriate classification applies instead.

evidence.pointer: Spellcasting feature descriptions and spell definitions

notes: Casting-time classification: most spells are 1 action; bonus-action spells and reaction spells have their own entries. Spells with longer casting times (1 minute+) use long-activation.
Bonus-action spell rule: if you cast a spell as a bonus action, you cannot cast another spell during the same turn except a cantrip with a casting time of 1 action.

name: Dash
mechanicalSummary: Gain extra movement equal to your speed for the current turn, after applying any modifiers.
notes: Cunning Action (Rogue L2) allows Dash as a bonus action. Monk's Step of the Wind allows Dash as a bonus action by spending 1 ki point.

name: Disengage
mechanicalSummary: Your movement does not provoke opportunity attacks for the rest of the turn.
notes: Cunning Action (Rogue L2) allows Disengage as a bonus action. Monk's Step of the Wind allows Disengage as a bonus action by spending 1 ki point.

name: Dodge
mechanicalSummary: Until the start of your next turn, attack rolls against you have disadvantage and you have advantage on Dexterity saving throws. You lose this benefit if incapacitated or your speed drops to 0.
notes: Monks can Dodge as a bonus action by spending 1 ki point (Patient Defense).

name: Help
mechanicalSummary: Choose an ally within 5 feet. That ally gains advantage on the next ability check it makes before your next turn, or on the next attack roll against a creature within 5 feet of you.
notes: The ally must be within 5 feet of you when you take the Help action.
For attack-roll help: the target creature must be within 5 feet of you.

name: Hide
mechanicalSummary: Make a Dexterity (Stealth) check to attempt to hide. You must be heavily obscured or have cover, and cannot be clearly seen. On success, you are hidden until discovered or you stop hiding.

evidence.pointer: Using Ability Scores — Dexterity — Stealth / Hiding

notes: Cunning Action (Rogue L2) allows Hide as a bonus action.

name: Ready
trigger: Player specifies a perceivable circumstance that will trigger the readied reaction
mechanicalSummary: Choose an action to ready and a perceivable trigger. When the trigger occurs, you can use your reaction to take the readied action or move up to your speed. A readied spell requires concentration and expends the slot when readied, not when released.
notes: Uses your reaction to execute. The readied action occurs after the trigger completes, unless otherwise stated.
Readied spells require concentration; the slot is lost if not released before the start of your next turn.

name: Search
mechanicalSummary: Devote your action to searching for something. Typically requires a Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) check.
notes: Inquisitive Rogue's Eye for Detail allows Search as a bonus action.

name: Use an Object
mechanicalSummary: Interact with an object that requires your action to use. This includes administering a potion, applying a poison, activating a magic item, or any other object interaction beyond the free object interaction.

evidence.pointer: Actions in Combat — Use an Object action

notes: Thief Rogue's Fast Hands allows Use an Object as a bonus action.
Most magic items use this action. Each item specifies if it requires an action, bonus action, or other activation.

name: Grapple
mechanicalSummary: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your weapon attacks with a grapple attempt. Make a Strength (Athletics) check contested by the target's Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics). On success, the target is grappled: its speed becomes 0. The grapple ends if the grappler is incapacitated or an effect moves the grappled creature out of reach.
notes: Grapple is NOT an independent action; it replaces one attack within the Attack action.
Requires a free hand. Size restriction: target must be no more than one size larger than you.
Grappler feat grants advantage on attacks against grappled targets and allows restraining a grappled creature.

name: Shove
mechanicalSummary: When you take the Attack action, you can replace one of your weapon attacks with a shove attempt. Make a Strength (Athletics) check contested by the target's Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics). On success, either knock the target prone or push it 5 feet away from you.

evidence.pointer: Actions in Combat — Shoving a Creature

notes: Shove is NOT an independent action; it replaces one attack within the Attack action.
Size restriction: target must be no more than one size larger than you.
Shield Master feat allows a shove as a bonus action after taking the Attack action.

name: Improvised Action
mechanicalSummary: Declare any action not covered by the standard actions. The DM adjudicates the outcome, potentially calling for an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw as appropriate.

evidence.pointer: Actions in Combat — Improvising an Action

notes: Catch-all for DM-adjudicated actions. Examples from the rules: intimidating an enemy, calling for a parley, interacting with environment features.

name: Opportunity Attack
trigger: A hostile creature that you can see moves out of your reach
mechanicalSummary: Use your reaction to make one melee weapon attack against the triggering creature. The attack occurs right before the creature leaves your reach.

evidence.pointer: Sentinel feat (in feats.json) modifies Opportunity Attack behavior

notes: Uses your reaction for the round. You get one reaction per round, regained at the start of your turn.
Disengage action prevents opportunity attacks for the rest of the turn.
Sentinel feat: hitting with an OA reduces the target's speed to 0, and you can make OAs even against creatures that Disengage.
War Caster feat allows casting a spell instead of making a weapon attack for an OA.
Polearm Master feat grants an OA when a creature enters your reach.

name: Two-Weapon Fighting
trigger: You take the Attack action and attack with a light melee weapon you are holding in one hand
mechanicalSummary: When you take the Attack action and attack with a light melee weapon in one hand, you can use a bonus action to attack with a different light melee weapon held in your other hand. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack unless that modifier is negative.
notes: Both weapons must have the Light property unless you have the Dual Wielder feat.
The Two-Weapon Fighting style (Fighter L1, Ranger L2) allows adding ability modifier to the bonus attack's damage.
Dual Wielder feat: +1 AC while dual wielding, can use non-light weapons, and can draw/stow two weapons.
